title: Scientists Find a Hidden World in Light
summary: Scientists in South Africa and China have discovered something amazing in light. They found hidden patterns in light that can hold information, like a secret code. These patterns are called topologies and can be very big, up to 48 dimensions, which is like having 48 different directions to store things. They used a tool called entangled photons, which are tiny particles of light that are connected. This discovery could help make computers and other devices work better by protecting information from mistakes. The best part is that many labs already have the equipment to see these patterns, so it's easy for others to use. This finding was published in a science journal and shows that light has more secrets than we thought.

background_read: Light is all around us and helps us see. Scientists study light to learn how it works and use it in things like computers and phones. Sometimes, light can have special properties, like being connected in ways we can't see. This article talks about a new discovery where scientists found hidden patterns in light that could make technology better by keeping information safe. It's like finding a secret code in something we use every day.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from South Africa and China
WHAT: Found hidden patterns called topologies in light that can store information
WHY: To help make quantum systems more reliable and protect information from noise

perspectives.description: This shows that even common tools in science can reveal new secrets, making it exciting for future research.
